stemHtml: A Training Captain who teaches other pilots how to deal with emergencies is more likely to be able to deal with a real emergency than one of the pilots who only practises emergencies during recurrent training. Which of the following correctly describes the relationship between skills and long term memory?

options.a: Long term memory is limited, and will become overwritten with newer skills.
options.b: Long term memory is practically unlimited. Skills are stored in the long term memory, but after not using the skills for a long time, retraining may be needed to access the skills.
options.c: The long term memory cannot store skills for more than 6 months, so that is the period at which recurrent training is required.
options.d: Skills are not held in the long term memory, instead they have a separate storage level in the brain which is unlimited and does not ever need to be revisited.
